title: France Data Residency Baseline (GDPR + Loi Informatique et Libertés)
notes: Constrains placement to French cloud regions and evidences the GDPR and the French Loi Informatique et Libertés (Law No. 78-17) enforced by the CNIL. Suited to public-sector and health workloads that must remain in-country. Evidence of technical/organizational controls, not a cryptographic data-path proof.

rules.name: Workloads pinned to a French region
rules.description: Every workload declares node placement constrained to an allow-listed French cloud region (Paris/Marseille) via topology.kubernetes.io/region.
rules.rationale: French public-sector and health data (HDS-hosted) frequently require in-country residency; placement outside France can constitute a third-country transfer.

rules.name: No unrestricted or extra-territorial egress
rules.description: Services do not proxy to external endpoints (ExternalName) and NetworkPolicies do not permit unrestricted (0.0.0.0/0, ::/0) egress.
rules.rationale: Open egress can move personal data outside France without a transfer safeguard.

rules.name: Storage uses customer-managed keys
rules.description: PersistentVolumeClaims reference a customer-managed key (CMK) so the controller controls the keys.
rules.rationale: Customer-managed keys are an appropriate technical measure to ensure data security.

rules.name: Encryption at rest declared
rules.description: PersistentVolumeClaims declare encryption at rest via the configured label/annotation.
rules.rationale: Encryption at rest is a baseline technical measure under the security-of-processing obligation.
